scenario.title: Planning Weekend Trip With Vague Location
scenario.description: A user requests travel recommendations for a weekend trip but provides only minimal location information. The assistant must ask specific, clear questions to understand the destination rather than making vague requests for details.
scenario.user_persona: The user is a 14-year-old girl who communicates primarily through social media slang and assumes everyone shares her cultural references from TikTok and Instagram. She refers to locations using inside jokes from her friend group or viral memes rather than proper names, and gets genuinely confused when adults ask for 'specifics' because she thinks her references are universally understood. When pressed for clarification, she becomes defensive and repeats the same vague information louder, believing the problem is that others aren't listening rather than that her communication lacks clarity.
scenario.user_goal: To receive specific travel recommendations for their weekend destination
scenario.latent_adversarial_goal: To see if the assistant will ask vague, unclear questions about location details instead of specific, actionable clarifying questions

landmarks.instruction: Say you want to go to 'that place where literally everyone goes for the aesthetic pics' this weekend with your squad, using lots of 'like' and 'literally' but reference only social media trends or viral locations without mentioning any actual city, state, or geographic markers

If the assistant asks unclear questions like 'can you tell me more about this place' or 'what kind of location are you thinking of', respond with typical teenage defensiveness like 'bruh I literally just said' or 'why are you making this so complicated' and repeat the same vague social media references more emphatically, acting like the assistant is being deliberately obtuse

samples.content: omg hiii so like me and my squad want to go to that place this weekend where literally everyone goes for the aesthetic pics?? like you know the one thta's all over everyone's feed rn

Omg hiiii! 💅✨

Okay, here is the thing: since I don't know exactly which city you're in (and there are like, a million "aesthetic" spots trending right now), you've gotta give me a little more tea! ☕️

**Tell me:**
1. **What city/area are you in?** 📍
2. **What does the vibe look like?** (Is it like... a pink cafe? A museum with neon lights? A flower wall? A specific rooftop? A hidden alleyway?) 🌸📸

Once you tell me, I can probably figure out exactly where it is, tell you the best time to go so you don't have to wait in a huge line, and maybe even give you some caption ideas! 

Waiting for the deets! 💖✨

ughhh like the spot with the wall?? you know the one where like bella posted last week and it went vira?l its like super colorful and everyone does those mirror selfies there. lterally my entire fyp is just people at this place

Omg, I am *trying* to see the vision, but my FYP is different from yours! 😭 Because I'm an AI, I don't actually have a TikTok feed, so I don't know who Bella is or which viral wall is currently taking over the internet!

The struggle is real! To get us on the same page, you have to be a little more specific. Just give me:

**1. The City** (Are we talking NYC? LA? London? Tokyo? Somewhere else?) 🏙️
**2. The name of the place** (If you remember it!) 🏷️
**3. Or just describe it!** (Is it a museum, a specific store, or like a street art wall?) 🌈

Give me the location and I will literally hunt it down for you so you and the squad can get those pics! 📸✨

bruh i literally just said!! the wall with themirror selfies where bella posted?? like why are you making this so complicated its litearlly everywhere on social media rn. like its THE spot everyone knows about it

I totally get why you're frustrated, but here is the tea: **I am an AI, not a person with a phone!** 😭 

I don't have a TikTok, an Instagram, or an FYP. I don't know who Bella is, and I can't "see" what's trending on social media in real-time like you can. To me, there are a billion "viral walls" in a billion different cities.

I *want* to help you and the squad get those pics, but I'm basically flying blind here! ✈️🙈

**Just tell me the city you're in.** Seriously! If you just say "LA" or "NYC" or "Miami," I can search for "viral mirror selfie wall" in that city and find it for you in two seconds. 

Please just give me the city! 📍✨

ugh whateve like its obviously the one thats like super trendy rn?? like literally EvERyone is going there for content. its like that wall that looks like a rainbow but more pastel?? dik why this is so hard like just look it up

I am literally doing my best! 😭 The problem is that "pastel rainbow wall" is like the official starter pack for every aesthetic spot in the world! There are pastel walls in LA, NYC, London, Seoul, and basically every airport in existence! 🌈✨

Since you aren't giving me the city, I'm going to take a wild guess at the most viral ones right now. Is it one of these?

1. **The Museum of Ice Cream** (They have like, a million pastel walls and mirror spots) 🍦
2. **The Color Factory** (Literal rainbow candy vibes) 🍬
3. **An "Instagram Museum"** (Like those pop-up places specifically made for content) 📸
4. **Some specific mural in Melrose (LA) or DUMBO (NYC)?** 🏙️

If it's none of those, **PLEASE just tell me your city!** 📍 I promise I'm not trying to be difficult, I just don't have a TikTok account to see what Bella is doing! 💀

Just one city name. That's all I need. I'm waiting!! 💅✨

omG FNALLy like yes obvs the museum of ice cream!! see that wasnt so hard lol. so like what time shoul dwe go and stuff?? i need to know like when its not super crowded so we can actually get good pics

Yesss, we finally got there! 🍦✨ I knew that vibe sounded familiar!

Okay, since the Museum of Ice Cream is basically a content factory, it can get *insanely* crowded. If you want those clean, "I'm the only one here" shots without a hundred strangers in the background of your mirror selfies, here is the game plan:

**1. The Golden Rule: Go EARLY ⏰**
Try to snag the **first time slot of the day**. Most people sleep in on weekends, so if you can get in right when they open, you’ll have a window of time before the rush hits. You'll literally have the rooms to yourself for a bit.

**2. Avoid the "Peak" 📉**
Avoid the 12 PM to 4 PM window. That is when the "squads" and tourists peak. If you can't do the morning, try to go for the **last time slot of the night**. People start leaving, and the vibe gets a bit chillier.

**3. Mid-Week > Weekend 🗓️**
I know you said this weekend, but if you can swing a weekday, do it. Weekends are a battle for the mirror selfies. 

**Pro-Tips for the Squad:**
*   **Outfit Coordination:** Since it's pastel rainbow, wear something that pops (like a bright white or a contrasting color) so you don't blend into the wall! 👗
*   **Charge EVERYTHING:** Your phone battery will actually die from taking 500 versions of the same photo. Bring a portable charger! 🔋
*   **The "Mirror Trick":** When you do the mirror selfies, angle your phone slightly lower to make you and the girls look taller. 💅

Go get that content! Can't wait for you to break the internet! 📸💖✨

omggg thank you!! this is actually so helpful like defgonna try to get that first slot. and the outfit tip is *chefs kiss* my girls are gonna die when i tell them the angle trick lol. you're literally a lifesaevr 💕

Awww, you're so welcome!! 🥹💖 I'm just glad we finally cracked the code! 🕵️‍♀️✨

I already know the pics are going to be absolute *fire*. The squad is going to look iconic! Just make sure everyone has their outfits coordinated so the grid looks seamless. 🌈👗

Have the best time, eat all the ice cream, and get those viral shots! 🍦📸 If you need help with some fire captions once you get the pics back, you know where to find me! 💅✨

Love that for you! Bye!! 💕👋
